candidates.why: This is COGS-related D&A, but it appears as a component beneath the selected COGS total rows. The presence of 'Cost of goods sold - Balancing value' indicates the selected COGS row already includes this amount, so adding it separately would double count.

variables.notes: Selected COGS rows are parent totals ('Cost of goods sold' / 'Cost of revenues/COGS'). The explicit COGS D&A rows are embedded components, evidenced by the balancing row, so separate addition would double count.

candidates.why: This row appears under both Selling expenses and Administrative expenses as a sub-component. The corresponding balancing rows indicate the selected parent SG&A component rows already include this depreciation, so it should not be added separately.

candidates.why: This row appears under both Selling expenses and Administrative expenses as a sub-component. The balancing rows indicate it is already embedded in the selected SG&A component rows, so adding it separately would double count.

This is a sub-component of the selected 'Research and development costs' row, as shown by the R&D balancing row. Also, R&D is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, so adding R&D D&A separately is not appropriate.

This is a sub-component of the selected 'Research and development costs' row, evidenced by the R&D balancing row. Since R&D is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, this should not be added separately.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows are parent operating expense rows ('Selling expenses', 'Administrative expenses', 'Research and development costs'). The D&A rows in SGA and R&D are embedded sub-components, supported by the balancing rows. R&D is also separately selected in XRD, reinforcing that R&D-related D&A should not be added again.
